parityMeta.describes: iOS-17e-0 progression parity scaffold — progressive-overload history. Five recent completed push-a sessions log the same main compound at a strictly rising top weight (60→62.5→65→67.5→70 kg) with NEUTRAL readiness and no training gap. The real TS trainingDecisionEngine reads these performed sets (isE1rmTrendUp=true over >=4 sessions, recoveryHigh=false) and produces the ADAPTIVE progressionMode='progress' / weeklyAdjustment output the static native template cannot. Same rising trend as controlled-reload-v1 but neutral (not low) readiness isolates the pure progression signal. DECODE-ONLY on the Swift side in 17e-0; compute-assert lands as 17e-1~5 port the progression cluster.
parityMeta.sourceNotes: Synthetic AppData. Five completed push-a sessions spaced 3 days (daysAgo 14/11/8/5/2), same exercise, strictly increasing top weight -> rising e1rm trend. Most recent 2 days before the clock (no gap -> normal phase persists). Default todayStatus keeps readiness out of the 'low' band, so recoveryHigh=false -> sessionIntent='normal-session' -> progressionMode='progress'.
